summary: Dropi Excel importer verified end-to-end. Backend unit tests (5) + HTTP tests (6) all pass. Frontend /import page uploaded a synthetic 4-row xlsx, showed Órdenes reales=2, Recaudo $235.000, amber multi-row warning, and committed imported=2 with 0 errors.
critical_code_review_comments: dropi.py preview endpoint stores full consolidated payload in Mongo (dropi_previews) with no TTL — could grow unbounded; consider a TTL index on created_at.

dropi_import.py collects `naive_sum` across ALL rows regardless of whether a group had multi-row combos; the reported naive-vs-correct delta is correct as-is, but the semantic is 'sum every row's TOTAL DE LA ORDEN' (which is what a naive importer would do) — fine, just document.
routes/dropi.py::dropi_import uses `carrier_by_slug` lookup but doesn't 400 when carriers collection is empty (only per-row error). Fine, but a preflight check would be a nicer UX.
Order.customer_phone fallback '+000000000' when phone missing may silently corrupt data — surface as an error instead of coercion.
seed_data_creation: Synthetic xlsx generated in-memory for each test run; unique IDs (timestamp-suffixed) to avoid dedup collisions between runs. Test orders remain in Mongo (external_ref prefix FE1/FE2/T*) — safe, no cleanup implemented.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Dropi importer specs from the review request are green. If retesting, remember the dedup step relies on external_ref/tracking_number so prior TEST_/FE* orders persist in Mongo and will show as skipped on re-runs — this is EXPECTED behaviour (validated by test_import_and_dedup).
